properties.page_content:
SNAIC Knowledge Base

Document Metadata

Primary entity: SIT x NVIDIA AI Centre

Short name: SNAIC

Official SIT name: AI Centre (in collaboration with NVIDIA)

Organisation type: Strategic collaboration between Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and NVIDIA

Launch date: April 2024

Official opening: October 2025

Location: SIT@Punggol, Block E2, Level 7, Room E2-07-12

Campus area: Punggol Digital District

Country: Singapore

Canonical Description

SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between SIT and NVIDIA. It helps companies adopt AI through applied research, AI solution development, and talent development. It also gives SIT students and professionals hands-on experience with real-world AI projects.

One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.

Mission

Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.

Vision

Bring the potential of AI into business operations so organisations can improve efficiency, intelligence, creativity, and innovation.

Core Problems SNAIC Helps Address

Problem 1: Technology barriers

Companies may face challenges related to data availability, cost, tooling, and technical complexity.

Problem 2: Computational resource barriers

Companies may lack access to suitable hardware, GPUs, or cloud compute.

Problem 3: Talent barriers

Companies may not have enough skilled, industry-ready AI talent.

SNAIC Solution Areas

Solution 1: Enhance domain knowledge

SNAIC combines AI expertise with industry domain knowledge.

Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI

SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems.

Solution 3: Cost-efficient AI solutions

SNAIC develops practical AI solutions that are aligned to business value and resource constraints.

Industry Sectors Supported

Healthcare

Education

Logistics

Finance

Manufacturing

Telecommunication

Transportation

Public sector and infrastructure

Strategic Pillars

Pillar 1: AI Application Development for Industry

SNAIC works with companies to co-develop practical AI solutions for real use cases.

Pillar 2: AI Talent Development

SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees.

Pillar 3: Translational AI Research

SNAIC conducts applied research that bridges academic innovation and industry implementation.

Collaboration Model

Model Summary

SNAIC follows a “1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies” model.

Model Benefits

End-to-end AI translation of impactful AI solutions

Co-supervision by SIT and NVIDIA experts

Increased AI adoption across industries

AI competency development for students and working professionals

Stronger AI talent pipeline

Key Resources

Resource Category: AI Talent

SIT AI Researchers, including professors, research fellows, and engineers

NVIDIA AI Researchers

Postgraduate students, including Industry Doctorate and Industry Master’s students

Undergraduate students

Resource Category: Key Technologies

Large Language Models (LLMs)

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

Multi-Modal Models

Natural Language Processing (NLP)

Computer Vision

Speech Technology

Resource Category: Compute Infrastructure

DGX H200

Quantity: 4 systems

Purpose: Large-scale model training and data processing

Typical uses: LLM training, computer vision, multimodal AI, generative AI

NVIDIA RTX A6000 Workstation

Purpose: Development, testing, and running AI applications

Typical uses: Model development, fine-tuning, inference, simulation, rendering

Machine Learning

Perception AI

Sentiment Analysis

AI for Digital Twins

Domain Areas

Healthcare and Biomedical

Education and Learning

Predictive and Generative Systems

Human-Centered and Interactive Systems

Industry and Manufacturing

Secure and Sustainable Systems

AI Technology Pillars

LLMs

RAG

Multimodal AI

NLP

Speech AI

Computer Vision

Training and Talent Programmes

Programme: IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme

Goal: Train more than 200 fresh graduates and mid-career professionals over 3 years to become AI practitioners.

Structure: 1. 2 months of intensive training in GenAI, LLMs, and Agentic AI. 2. 4 months of work on real-life projects with industry partners.

Outcome: Participants gain current AI skills, practical project experience, and exposure to industry use cases.

Programme: Applied AI Doctoral Training Centre

Goal: Train 10 industrial doctorate students annually on complex, industry-driven challenges.

Project type: Multi-year research projects that cannot be solved with off-the-shelf tools.

Role in talent pathway: Creates a pathway from AI practitioner to applied AI researcher and innovator.

Talent Pathway Summary

The IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme and the Applied AI Doctoral Training Centre together form a tiered AI talent development pathway.

Operating Areas

Admin and Documentation

Admin

Documentation

Guides

People Operations

Employee management for staff, stakeholders, students, assistants, and interns

Onboarding

Performance

Offboarding and handover

Recruitment

Job description publishing

CV retrieval

Screening

Interview

Offer

Projects and Research

Research

Development

Project management

Grants

Overseas university exchange and internships

AI Centre Space and Infrastructure

Renovation

Lab safety

Networking and internet access

Resource access and maintenance

Network

DGX systems

Collaboration Process with SNAIC

Process Overview

The collaboration process has 6 stages: 1. Pre-engagement questionnaire 2. 1-to-1 consultation 3. Project scoping 4. Resource identification 5. Project development 6. Project deployment or scaling

Step 1: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire

Step name: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire

Purpose: Understand the company’s needs and objectives.

What the company does: Fill in a questionnaire.

What SNAIC does with the information: - Reviews the problem statement - Matches the problem statement to SNAIC capabilities - Assigns the right Technical Lead for the first consultation

Output of this step: Initial understanding of needs and assigned Technical Lead.

Step 2: 1-to-1 Consultation

Step name: 1-to-1 Consultation

Purpose: Explore whether AI can address the company’s problem statements.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC and the company have an in-depth discussion - They identify where AI solutions can create significant value - They clarify possible use cases and expectations

Output of this step: Initial alignment on business problem, AI opportunities, and expected value.

Step 3: Project Scoping

Step name: Project Scoping

Purpose: Define the roadmap for integrating AI into operations.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC AI experts work with the company - Project milestones are defined - Deliverables are defined - The AI adoption roadmap is outlined

Output of this step: Project roadmap, milestones, and deliverables.

Step 4: Resource Identification

Step name: Resource Identification

Purpose: Determine the resources, expertise, budget, and funding mechanism needed.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC and the company identify required talent, technology, and budget - Both parties align on a suitable funding mechanism - Both parties confirm resource contributions

Possible funding mechanisms: - Company-funded project - Joint grant application by company and SIT - Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s project - IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme - Integrative Team Project (ITP)

SNAIC resource contribution may include: - AI talent from SIT and NVIDIA - Postgraduate students - Undergraduate students - Key AI technologies - NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources

Industry partner contribution may include: - Domain experts - Problem statements - Available data - Funding

Output of this step: Agreed resources, funding route, and role allocation.

Step 5: Project Development

Step name: Project Development

Purpose: Build and refine an AI solution.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC works closely with the company team - A minimum viable project (MVP) is developed - AI models are selected - AI models are customised and refined - The solution is tailored to the company’s needs and objectives

Output of this step: Working MVP and refined AI solution design.

Step 6: Project Deployment or Scaling

Step name: Project Deployment or Scaling

Purpose: Move the MVP into production or expand it.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC advances the MVP into a production-ready solution - The solution is deployed or scaled - SNAIC provides continuous support

Output of this step: Production-ready solution or scaled deployment with ongoing support.

Example Projects by Technology Area

LLMs, Healthcare AI, and Digital Biology

LionNutriAI, AI nutrition coaching agent for precision nutrition assessment

Automated Visual Acuity Testing, AI eyesight testing automation

Pro-GO, de novo protein generation with controllable properties

Enhancing Ultrasound Image Reconstruction, distributed continual learning for ultrasound image reconstruction

RAG, Productivity, and Automation

AIDISA, AI-driven intelligent situation awareness system

ForumAID, AI-enhanced LMS to support teaching and learning in SIT

3D Content Creation, VLM for enhancing 3D scene creation

GENESIS, generative AI-aided safety investigation system

Multimodal Models and Personified AI

AI Debator, AI agent for structured healthcare debate

Unified RAG, enhanced multimodal RAG for complex information systems

Feature Fusion, framework for diverse multimodal feature fusion

Real-Time Video Analytics

Intelligent Visual Surveillance system, AI-powered safety surveillance in smart factories

Multimodal video surveillance platform, transforms CCTV into an intelligent security platform

Speech Technology and NLP

Speech Enhancement, improving Whisper for children’s speech recognition in Malay

Enhancing Speaker Anonymisation, real-time speaker verification and anonymisation systems

Speaker Emotion Recognition, model adaptation for multilingual speaker emotion recognition

Computer Vision and Digital Twins

Digital Expert for Fiduciary Analysis, enhanced MCP with finance RAG, deep research, persona, and explainable AI

AI-Powered Fluid Simulations for Digital Twin, faster simulations with physics-informed neural networks

SynthDa, synthetic data generation for human action recognition

Networks and Security

AI for Cyber, LLM for binary decompilation and vulnerability analysis

XAI for Data Poisoning, explainable AI for mitigation of data poisoning in LAGs and LLMs

AQM with AI, AI active queue management for network congestion control

ISAC with AI, AI for integrated sensing and communication in wireless networks

Progress Since Launch in April 2024

Approximately 70 companies engaged

Collaborations with more than 20 companies

More than 50 industry AI projects

19 Industrial Doctorate and Industry Master’s students

20 faculty supervisors

More than 150 undergraduates

Sample Partner Organisations

Healthcare and Life Sciences

Tan Tock Seng Hospital

Centre for Healthcare Innovation

Industrial and Manufacturing

Procter & Gamble

MERRY

Hitachi

JAPFA

Commercial and Digital Services

Prudential

Hepha.AI

StarHub

Public Sector and Infrastructure

SMRT Corporation

ST Engineering

Republic of Singapore Navy

Infocomm Media Development Authority

IDEMIA Public Security

People’s Association

Education and Research

Nanyang Technological University, Singapore

Republic Polytechnic

Chulalongkorn University

University of Economics and Law

Monash University

Frequently Asked Questions

FAQ 1: Can a participant work and study during the IMDA AI Programme?

The communicated position is that participants may join if they take no-pay leave for 6 months. This avoids overlap between full-time salary, the IMDA incentive, and normal company work during training.

FAQ 2: Can a participant use SkillsFuture or other government subsidies for the IMDA AI Programme?

No. The programme is already funded by IMDA. Other government subsidies should not be used because that would amount to double dipping.
properties.summary: SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between Singapore Institute of Technology and NVIDIA launched in April 2024, aiming to accelerate AI adoption through applied research, talent development, and industry solution co‑creation. Its three missions focus on advancing AI adoption via research, supporting industry capability and AI application development, and training skilled personnel for local industries. SNAIC operates under three pillars—AI application development, AI talent development, and translational AI research—supported by DGX H200 systems, AI expertise, and a talent pipeline comprising undergraduates, postgraduates, and industry professionals. Since launch, it has engaged around 70 companies, delivered over 50 AI projects, and trained dozens of industrial doctorate and master’s students alongside hundreds of undergraduates. The collaboration follows a "1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies" model, providing end‑to‑end AI translation, co‑supervision, and scalable deployment pathways.
